premise: My husband’s son from his first marriage was the lawyer for our farm-to-table restaurant, and after my husband died suddenly, he forged documents to claim he owned 80% of the business, liquidated $300,000 in assets, and left me with just the farmhouse and a mountain of debt. I spent five years auditing every transaction until I found the forgery, reported him to the state bar and got him disbarred, then bought the restaurant’s debt at auction for $18,000, forced a bankruptcy sale, and repurchased the business.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Stepson Forged Documents, Stole $300K, Left Me. So I Bought The Debt And Watc
